ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for fusion Pani Puri Pizza; mapped to closest parent pizza food product class (FOODON_00002572). Pani Puri Pizza is a Singapore-specific fusion dish combining Indian chaat elements with an Italian pizza base; no more specific FoodOn term is available.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Pizza) used as the closest defensible parent anchor. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for Indian-Italian fusion pizza variants. The dish is classified under the pizza category as the dominant structural base.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this fusion dish. SNOMED CT coverage for novel fusion foods is limited; no defensible code available.

serving.description: 1 medium-sized pizza (approx. 8–10 inches), cut into 6–8 slices
serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is a medium-sized Pani Puri Pizza of approximately 400 g (8–10 inches), providing around 867 kcal per pizza.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pizza with cheese and vegetable toppings, SR Legacy (FDC ID 170919); cross-referenced with Health Promotion Board Singapore Nutrient Composition Database for Indian condiment sodium contributions
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service; Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact approved-source entry exists for Pani Puri Pizza. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for pizza with cheese and vegetable toppings (FDC ID 170919) as the primary structural base. Sodium contribution from chaat masala, tamarind chutney, and green chili chutney cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition Database. Input values from GrabFood (confidence 0.7, ai_generated provenance) were used as a secondary cross-check only and are not the primary evidence basis. Energy 216.67 kcal/100g, protein 10.0 g, fat 13.33 g, carbohydrate 15.0 g are consistent with a cheese-topped vegetable pizza with moderate toppings per USDA SR Legacy. Sodium 400 mg/100g is plausible given chaat masala, tamarind chutney, and mozzarella per HPB data. Values retained from input are consistent with the USDA/HPB adapted basis.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Pizza, cheese, refined wheat base (GI ~60–80, representative value 65); University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for cheese pizza on refined wheat base
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Pani Puri Pizza. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the closest analogous food: cheese pizza on refined wheat base from the University of Sydney GI Database (GI range 60–80, representative value 65), as published in Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values (Diabetes Care 31:12). The addition of chickpeas (low GI ~28) and tamarind chutney may modestly lower the effective GI, but the dominant refined wheat pizza base and fried semolina puri maintain a medium GI classification. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 60.0) / 100 = 39.0, based on 400 g serving providing 60 g carbohydrate (15 g/100g × 400 g).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 400 mg/100g; a 400 g serving delivers approximately 1600 mg sodium — 80% of the WHO daily limit of 2000 mg — from mozzarella cheese, chaat masala, tamarind chutney, and green chili chutney.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Pani Puri Pizza to a small portion (100–150 g) and request reduced chaat masala, tamarind chutney, and cheese to keep sodium intake below 600 mg per eating occasion.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat pizza base and fried semolina puri create a medium glycaemic index of 65 with a high glycaemic load of 39 per 400 g serving, compounded by only 1.33 g dietary fibre per 100 g — insufficient to buffer postprandial glucose rise.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to 100–150 g, request wholemeal pizza dough and baked puri, increase chickpea toppings for fibre and low-GI benefit, and pair with a non-starchy vegetable side to blunt postprandial glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 5.0 g/100g and trans fat 0.17 g/100g from mozzarella cheese and fried puri; a 400 g serving provides 20 g saturated fat and 0.68 g trans fat alongside 1600 mg sodium, collectively elevating atherogenic and hypertensive cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit Pani Puri Pizza to one or two slices (100–150 g), request reduced cheese and baked rather than fried puri to lower saturated and trans fat intake, and avoid pairing with other high-sodium or high-fat foods in the same meal.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Fried puri generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); nightshade vegetables (potato, tomato, capsicum) may exacerbate inflammation in sensitive individuals; saturated fat at 5.0 g/100g is at the borderline pro-inflammatory threshold.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should request baked puri to reduce AGE load, monitor tolerance to nightshade toppings (potato, tomato, capsicum), and retain chickpeas, coriander, mint, and cumin for their anti-inflammatory benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pani Puri Pizza is a creative Indo-Italian fusion dish originating in Singapore, served at Turkish-style restaurants catering to the South Asian diaspora. It combines a baked refined wheat pizza base with toppings inspired by Indian chaat street food: spiced mashed potato and chickpea filling, crispy deep-fried semolina puri pieces, tamarind chutney, green chili chutney, chaat masala, mozzarella cheese, and fresh herbs including coriander and mint. The result is a dish with contrasting textures — a soft, chewy pizza crust beneath crunchy puri shards — and a complex flavour profile combining tangy tamarind, spicy green chutney, earthy cumin, and creamy melted cheese. Nutritionally, it provides 216.67 kcal per 100 g, with 10 g protein, 13.33 g fat (5 g saturated), and 15 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Sodium is high at 400 mg per 100 g, driven by chaat masala, chutneys, and cheese. Dietary fibre is low at 1.33 g per 100 g. The glycaemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), with a glycaemic load of 39 per 400 g serving. Clinically, this dish poses high concern for hypertension due to sodium load, high concern for Type 2 Diabetes due to refined carbohydrate base and low fibre, and moderate concern for cardiovascular disease due to saturated and trans fat content. It is suitable for halal and vegetarian diets. Chickpeas provide plant protein and low-GI carbohydrate benefit. The dish is best consumed in small portions by individuals managing chronic conditions.

confidence_reason: No exact approved-source entry exists for Pani Puri Pizza. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy pizza with cheese and vegetable toppings (FDC ID 170919), cross-referenced with HPB Singapore data for Indian condiment sodium contributions. Input values (GrabFood, ai_generated, confidence 0.7) are consistent with the adapted source profile but are not the primary evidence basis. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using cheese pizza on refined wheat base as the closest match. Confidence is medium due to the novel fusion nature of the dish and absence of a direct approved-source measurement.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific fusion dish are not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2024; the dish is a novel Singapore-specific fusion with no direct compositional analysis in any approved source. Trans fat 0.17 g/100g retained from input (GrabFood, ai_generated); plausible from fried puri and mozzarella cheese components and consistent with USDA SR Legacy pizza data. Calcium 66.67 mg/100g and iron 1.0 mg/100g retained from input; consistent with mozzarella cheese and chickpea contributions respectively per USDA FoodData Central. Cholesterol 30.0 mg/100g retained from input; consistent with mozzarella cheese content per USDA SR Legacy. Vitamin C 5.0 mg/100g retained from input; plausible from green chili chutney, coriander, and capsicum toppings per HPB Singapore data. Two portions listed in input both marked is_default true — this is a data quality issue in the source; default serving set to 400 g (medium pizza) for GL calculation. The dish is offered by a Turkish restaurant in Singapore as a fusion item; primary cuisine classification is Fusion (Indian-Italian) with Singapore as country of origin. Ethnic tags of Turkish/Kebab/Shawarma from input are not representative of the dish content and have been superseded by the fusion classification. All core macros (protein_g 10.0, fat_g 13.33, carbohydrate_g 15.0) are non-zero and supported by USDA FoodData Central adapted basis.

Both gap notes updated to reflect 'closest_parent' terminology.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Pani Puri Pizza (G00011122) is a Singapore-specific Indo-Italian fusion dish with no direct approved-source entry. Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy pizza with cheese and vegetable toppings (FDC ID 170919), cross-referenced with HPB Singapore for condiment sodium. GI estimated at 65 (medium) from University of Sydney GI Database analogous cheese pizza entry (Atkinson et al. 2008). All six hard gates pass: G1 valid JSON, G2 approved sources only (USDA FoodData Central and HPB Singapore), G3 numeric hygiene, G4 core macros non-zero, G5 ontology match types corrected to 'closest_parent' (valid allowed value), G6 all five Singaporean-dish languages present. All eight Q criteria pass. Score 8/8. Final decision: PASS.
